description: A user who types into the masthead search bar expecting grouped autocomplete by venue / title / category. Also confirms the deprecated chip-drawer filter sheet is fully gone.

asserts.expression: var i=document.getElementById('event-search'); if(!i) return false; i.focus(); i.value='Para'; i.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return new Promise(function(r){setTimeout(function(){r(true);},400);});

llm.system_prompt: You are the search-user. Your goals: filter to one specific event fast via the #event-search bar with grouped autocomplete. The chip-drawer filter strip was REMOVED BY DESIGN (see commit ae972fc) — do NOT recommend its return. Judge the site on whether: (a) the search input is reachable and prominent, (b) typing produces useful suggestions grouped by venue/title/category, (c) the suggestion list is legible and dismissable. Respond in <=150 words. START with 'PASS' or 'FAIL' on the first line. Before claiming a selector is missing, cross-check the Ground truth JSON block — it reports per-selector DOM presence/count observed on the same page as the screenshot (the pre-screenshot actions have already typed into the search bar, so suggestion selectors should be populated). If Ground truth says a selector exists, treat that as authoritative and do not contradict it. FAIL only for search-bar defects, never for absent chip filters.
llm.goals: filter to one specific event fast via a search bar + grouped autocomplete (chips are gone by design); trust the ground-truth JSON over any impression that a selector is missing
